mission.title: knative: Support Sidecar Processes
mission.description: Support Sidecar Processes. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the feature

It would be useful to allow developers to run additional sidecar processes next to their application code.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Sidecar Processes
steps.description:
I'd like to also better understand how the two containers relate, and why two containers are better than two processes in one container.

1. What happens if one container dies and not the other?
2. One container will be scaled and driven by requests. If we turned off the CPU between requests, would
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: test
spec:
  volumes:
  - name: sock
    emptyDir: {}
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: test
    imagePullPolicy: Never
    command: ["/usr/sbin/nginx", "-g daemon off;"]
    volumeMounts:
      - name: sock
        mountPath: /sock/
  - name: php-fpm
    image: test
    imagePullPolicy: Never
    command: ["php-fpm"]
    volumeMounts:
      - name: sock
        mountPath: /sock/
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Sidecar Processes" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I'd like to also better understand how the two containers relate, and why two containers are better than two processes in one container.

1. What happens if one container dies and not the other?
2. One container will be scaled and driven by requests. If we turned off the CPU between requests, would that cause a problem?
3. Do you need any sort of shared disk/etc between the two processes?
4.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: test
spec:
  volumes:
  - name: sock
    emptyDir: {}
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: test
    imagePullPolicy: Never
    command: ["/usr/sbin/nginx", "-g daemon off;"]
    volumeMounts:
      - name: sock
        mountPath: /sock/
  - name: php-fpm
    image: test
    imagePullPolicy: Never
    command: ["php-fpm"]
    volumeMounts:
      - name: sock
        mountPath: /sock/

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
